items: Me: I'll just rest for 5 minutes. Also me: *wakes up 3 hours later*
My diet starts Monday. Also me on Monday: My diet starts next Monday.
Telling myself 'one more episode' for the 7th time tonight.
When the alarm goes off and you remember it's actually your day off.
My brain at 2am: Remember that embarrassing thing from 10 years ago? Let's discuss.
Payday: *arrives* Me and my wallet: We're rich! Three days later: Broke again.
Me: I'm going to sleep early tonight. My bed at 11pm: LOL okay.
My stomach: I'm hungry. My brain: We just ate. My stomach: We did not agree to this.
When you finally clean your room and trip over nothing in the dark anyway.
Me: I'll do it tomorrow. Tomorrow me: I'll do it tomorrow.
The face you make when someone says 'just a quick meeting' and it's 2 hours long.
Telling myself I'll nap for 20 minutes and waking up as a new person in a new era.
My to-do list: 47 tasks. Me: Organizes the list instead of doing the tasks.
When you're at the restaurant and you pick the wrong thing off the menu.
Checking your phone immediately after putting it down like something changed.
My confidence level: Answering questions in my head during the meeting vs. out loud.
Me saving 20% on groceries then spending it on snacks I didn't need.
When someone says 'let's keep it simple' and then explains for 30 minutes.
My weekend plan vs. my actual weekend: Sleep, snack, repeat.
Me ordering a salad: I'm so healthy. Me adding extra cheese and croutons: Balance.
Buying something on sale you didn't need because 'it was too good to pass up.'
When the Wi-Fi goes down and you have to talk to your family like it's 1995.
Me to me: Stop overthinking. Also me: But what if I analyze it just one more time?
The five stages of Mondays: Denial, anger, bargaining, more sleep, finally getting up.
Walking into work on a Monday like I didn't just have two days off.
My wallet crying when I enter any store I didn't plan to go into.
Exercise goal: Run 5km. Reality: Walk to the fridge and back. Still counts.
Me: I'll just check social media for 5 minutes. Three hours later: Where did I go?
Nobody: My brain at 3am: Did I lock the door? Let's check 17 times.
Watching one cooking video then walking into the kitchen like a professional chef.
Pretending I know what I'm doing in life while googling everything.
Me finishing a bag of chips I wasn't even hungry for: We move.
Brain: You should sleep. Also brain: Let me replay every awkward moment from 2014.
The look of confidence I put on every Monday pretending this week will be different.
My face when someone says 'You look tired' — thanks, I did not consent to that feedback.
Being productive for 20 minutes then taking a 2-hour victory nap.
Me buying gym clothes instead of actually going to the gym. Same energy.
Going to bed at 9pm feeling like a champion. Waking up at 5am confused.
That feeling when you rehearsed the whole conversation but it went a completely different way.
Meeting someone new: I'm very calm and collected. My brain: Say something weird. NOW.
When you say yes to plans two weeks out thinking Future You will cancel them.
My anxiety making up problems that don't exist since before I could walk.
Trying to adult: Step 1. What is step 2? Nobody told me about step 2.
Me at the grocery store with a list: buys everything except what's on the list.
The joy of opening a new notebook. The sadness when I have to write in it.
My social battery after talking to two people: Please recharge me.
Starting a new show: I'll just watch one episode. Me, 4 hours later: What year is it?
My level of tired: So tired I'm describing how tired I am to anyone who'll listen.
Me preparing a whole speech in my head that I will never actually deliver.
Walking into the kitchen for a reason then forgetting why I came in.
Confidence: 100% in the mirror. 0% when someone actually looks at me.
My philosophy: If it still needs to be done tomorrow, it wasn't really urgent today.
Work email at 5:01 PM: Just a quick question... Me: I am unavailable for life now.
When you finally finish cleaning and something immediately makes a mess.
My screen time report: 7 hours on phone. Me: That seems low actually.
The voice in my head when I order food: You deserve this. The voice after: Do you, though?
Making a whole grocery run but forgetting the one thing you actually needed.
Me: This week I'll cook every day. Me on Wednesday: Delivery again, but make it healthy.
When someone asks 'What are you thinking about?' and the truth is absolutely unhinged.
My budget at the beginning of the month vs. the end: A tragedy in two acts.
Adult life in a nutshell: Be tired. Pay bills. Repeat. Find joy wherever possible.
My phone battery: 100% when I start a movie. 3% before the ending.
Being absolutely convinced I'd remember something without writing it down. I did not.
The satisfaction of crossing one thing off a 47-item to-do list.
Starting a diet on Tuesday because Monday already felt too soon.
When someone sends you a 'we need to talk' and your heart relocates to your stomach.
My self-control when walking past a bakery: This is not my home. I will keep walking. I have turned around.
Late nights: Convincing myself I work better under pressure is not a personality trait.
When you finally understand the instructions after it's already too late.
Doing mental math at the store while looking totally calm on the outside.
Promising myself I'll respond to that message later. Three months ago.
That one friend who always says 'almost there' when they haven't left the house.
The motivation I have at 10pm to change my life vs. at 7am when the alarm goes off.
Me: I'll start saving money this month. Online sale: Hello.
The energy I have scrolling at midnight vs. the energy I have getting up for work.
Friday me vs. Monday me: Completely different people. One of them hasn't slept.
Choosing the self-checkout lane to avoid talking to anyone, then needing assistance.
My body at 7am: We are NOT doing this. My body after coffee: Let us conquer the world.
Every time I say 'this is the last time' and it is absolutely not the last time.
My sleep schedule: A myth my bed refuses to honor.
Me saying 'I'll just look at this store for a second': Two bags later.
Standing in front of a full fridge for 10 minutes saying there's nothing to eat.
My brain has two settings: Overthinking everything and completely forgetting the obvious.
Procrastinator's creed: Why do today what can be done when the panic sets in?
Me learning a new skill: Day 1 — I'm a genius. Day 2 — I am struggling. Day 3 — I quit.
My memory when I need something: Gone. My memory when I'm trying to sleep: Fully operational.
That feeling when your weekend is more exhausting than your work week.
When someone cancels plans: Devastated. Also me: Genuinely relieved.
Being on time everywhere except where I actually need to be.
My motivation to clean: Zero. My ability to notice the mess: Infinite.
When you write your name on your lunch and someone eats it anyway: War.
My fitness journey: I exercised today. That's it. That's the whole journey.
Me saying 'I'm almost ready' when I haven't started getting ready yet.
Every single Monday: New week, new me. Same me, new Monday.
My inner voice when I'm about to say something smart: Don't. My inner voice: Say it.
Going to bed telling myself tomorrow will be different. Tomorrow: It was the same.
Waking up from a nap not knowing if it's AM or PM or which century we're in.
The two types of tired: So tired I can sleep immediately. So tired I can't sleep at all.
When you buy something and immediately see it cheaper somewhere else.
My plan: Get up early, exercise, eat healthy. My execution: Sleep in, eat snacks, repeat.
Being 100% sure I left my keys somewhere logical. They were in my hand the whole time.
Starting conversations with 'So anyway' like I didn't spend 10 minutes planning that opening.
My wallet and my taste in restaurants have never agreed on anything.
Waiting for 'the right time' to do something as if perfect conditions actually exist.
When someone says 'I'll keep it brief' and then goes for 45 minutes.
Buying a planner to organize my life. Forgetting to use the planner. Classic.
The confidence of typing an email, then rereading it five times before hitting send.
My brain: Store this memory. Also my brain: Deleting it in 3 seconds.
When I say 'I'll be there in 5' it's a spiritual estimate, not a factual one.
Meal prepping on Sunday like a responsible adult. Eating cereal for dinner on Wednesday.
Signing up for the gym in January. Becoming a regular in March. Disappearing by April.
When someone asks for 'just a moment of your time' and you lose 45 minutes.
The bravery it takes to look at a bank statement after a week of online shopping.
Me at 10pm: I should go to bed early. Me at 2am: One more video.
Being the person who remembers everyone's birthday and forgets their own responsibilities.
Promising myself I'll just have 'a little bit' of dessert. It was not 'a little bit.'
When you're exhausted but somehow can't stop watching your favorite show.
My eating habits when no one's watching vs. when someone is: Completely different human.
Me making the same mistake again: This is called experience. You're welcome.
Realizing I've been saying I'll 'start fresh on Monday' for approximately 3 years.
Finishing a great book and staring at the wall for 20 minutes processing.
My 3am thoughts: Let me remind you of every wrong thing you've ever done.
The commitment I give to rearranging my desk instead of doing actual work.
When the delivery rider says 'almost there' and it's been 40 minutes.
My phone's brightness at max in a dark room: A personal attack.
Telling myself 'just five more minutes' as if I don't know exactly what's going to happen.
When the playlist is so good the workout accidentally becomes an extra 30 minutes.
Treating Google like the friend who knows everything.
The confidence I type with versus the confidence I speak with: Completely different people.
Me: I'm going to be so productive today. Also me: *5 hours of YouTube later*
Adult problems nobody warned you about: Picking a good avocado is genuinely stressful.
The moment you sit down after a long day and the remote is just out of reach.
Me and my alarm clock: Enemies, but in a complicated relationship.
Dreaming about all the things I'd do if I had more time, while wasting the time I have.
Setting 5 alarms and then sleeping through all of them plus a sixth one I forgot about.
When someone says 'low-key' and proceeds to describe something intensely extra.
Grocery shopping with no list: A dangerous game I always lose.
The version of me who makes weekend plans and the version who actually shows up are different people.
Me: I'm not going to check my phone. Also me: *checks phone before finishing that sentence*
The look on my face pretending I knew about something I'm hearing for the first time.
Eating food I'm not hungry for because it's just sitting there.
Going to bed early and then having the best thoughts ever at midnight.
My self-control at a buffet: A carefully maintained illusion.
The deep sigh I let out when I finally get home and take off my shoes.
Me at the start of every week: This is MY week. Me by Thursday: Next week.
Telling yourself you're just resting your eyes and waking up 3 hours later.
When you're on time for once in your life and the event is running late.
Buying coffee every day to save money on medical bills from the exhaustion of adulting.
Being on a roll then getting a notification and losing the entire train of thought.
The way I eat salad at home vs. at a restaurant: Like two completely different diets.
My face when someone calls instead of texting: I was not mentally prepared for this.
Making eye contact with someone and immediately forgetting every word in every language.
Sending a text and instantly rereading it to see if it could be misinterpreted. It could.
My to-do list: Exists. Me: Acknowledges existence. Me: Does not do list.
The confidence I have dancing alone vs. when someone's watching: Zero overlap.
Me promising myself I'll go to bed at a reasonable hour: The fourth lie of the day.
The internal conflict of wanting to go out and also never leaving the house ever again.
When you finally complete a task you've been avoiding for weeks: CHAMPION.
My personality when I'm hungry: Completely different species.
Being absolutely certain I know a shortcut that takes twice as long.
The drama I have in my head about conversations that will never happen.
Me: Let me just check one thing. Two hours later: I have solved nothing but watched everything.
When the weekend flies by and Monday ambushes you like it's never happened before.
Running a little late. Also me: Running was not involved.
The way I respond to 'how are you' vs. how I actually am: Completely different answers.
Me at 7am regretting every decision that led to this moment.
When autocorrect changes something embarrassing to something even more embarrassing.
Spending 45 minutes deciding what to watch then not watching anything.
The peace I feel when the house is finally quiet and everyone is asleep.
Optimistic me setting 5 alarms. Realistic me sleeping through all 5.
My financial plan: Work. Spend. Regret. Repeat. Save theoretically.
Hitting the gym once and checking if there are visible results. There were not.
The strength it takes to not respond immediately when you clearly already read the message.
My relationship with deadlines: Adversarial but ultimately productive.
Waking up to an alarm and bargaining with yourself like the alarm has feelings.
My inner voice telling me to send the email already. I have been drafting it for two days.
Finding motivation at 11pm to do things I've postponed since the previous Monday.
Me: I need a nap. Also me after nap: I need another nap.
Trying to read the menu before the waiter comes back. I never manage this.
Bringing snacks on a road trip and finishing them within the first hour.
The face I make when asked to do 'just one more thing' at the end of the day.
Realizing the thing I've been stressing about for days took 10 minutes to resolve.
The level of joy I feel when plans get cancelled: Immeasurable and I feel no shame.
Me returning to a hobby I abandoned three months ago: We meet again.
The inner monologue while pretending to understand something: I'll figure it out later.
Making dinner plans and then spending 20 minutes saying 'I don't know, what do YOU want?'
My energy on Saturday at 10pm vs. Sunday at 10pm: Completely different timelines.
Going to the gym for the first time in a month and limping for three days.
Phone at 100% when I leave the house. Phone at 4% by noon. It's personal.
When someone asks 'Are you almost ready?' — a question with no safe answer.
Me planning all the things I'll do during my time off. Me during time off: Sleep and snacks.
The look I give when someone says something that doesn't add up but I'm too tired to argue.
Ordering food delivery then tracking it like I'm monitoring a space mission.
Me talking myself into a purchase: You've been so good. You deserve this.
A clean desk: Exists for exactly 20 minutes after I tidy it.
The satisfaction of peeling plastic off a new gadget: Pure and unmatched.
Getting to the end of a very important email and forgetting what I was going to say.
Thinking about saying something in a meeting and then someone else says it before me.
That one song that puts you in an entirely different emotional universe.
The internal celebration when someone cancels plans I didn't want to attend anyway.
My morning self making promises my night self has no intention of keeping.
Standing outside the door practicing what I'll say then completely improvising.
Me explaining a life plan at 25 that has literally not happened yet.
My productivity right before a deadline: Supernatural.
Taking a shower and solving every problem I've ever had. Remembering none of them.
Finding the thing I've been looking for in the last place I looked. Every time.
Me buying an 'emergency snack' that somehow disappeared by the time the emergency arrived.
Forgetting why I walked into a room for the 400th time today.
The moment I know exactly what to say — right after the conversation is over.
My planner in January vs. my planner in March: Two entirely different life philosophies.
Starting ten things and finishing zero of them and calling it a productive day.
My laptop dying the exact second I didn't save my work: A love story.
Saying 'I'll clean that later' about something I have been looking at for two weeks.
Me confidently going the wrong direction and pretending it was intentional.
When you finally go grocery shopping and immediately cook nothing you bought.
The optimism of making a to-do list the night before and the reality of the next morning.
Reaching for my phone the moment I experience any emotion whatsoever.
My cooking: Looks nothing like the recipe. Tastes okay. I'll take it.
When someone says something rude and my brain delivers the perfect comeback three days later.
The relief of finishing a difficult task followed immediately by the next difficult task.
Me checking the weather and then dressing for a completely different forecast.
The distance between 'I should exercise' and 'I am exercising' is somehow enormous.
Opening the fridge and then closing it for the third time like something changed.
Starting a sentence without knowing how it will end and hoping everyone goes with it.
The confidence of ordering something ambitious at a restaurant: Justified. Sometimes.
Me when I have one task: Overwhelmed. Me when I have ten tasks: Operating in crisis mode.
The false sense of productivity I get from color-coding my notes.
Waking up 5 minutes before my alarm and not knowing if I should sleep or just wait.
Me looking for something in plain sight for 10 minutes: Advanced skills.
Talking to yourself quietly at the store to figure out what you came in for.
The way a to-do list grows the moment you cross one thing off.
Me explaining to myself why I needed the thing I just bought online at midnight.
The wild confidence I have between 11pm and 2am that disappears at 7am.
Finishing leftovers from the fridge while standing with the door open: The experience.
Deciding to be productive while immediately opening a different tab.
Becoming a completely different person the moment music goes in my ears.
The specific embarrassment of walking back past someone after going the wrong way.
My determination at the beginning of a task vs. my determination at the middle.
The mystery of where the time went when I 'just sat down for a minute.'
Me setting alarms for everything, including things that don't need alarms.
That specific calm of finally doing the thing you've been avoiding: Inexplicable peace.
When you miss a call and it's simultaneously a tragedy and a relief.
The bravery it takes to look in the camera for a video call versus the reality.
Me watching one documentary and becoming briefly certain I'm now an expert.
Every January: New year, new habits. Every February: Habits were a phase.
The very specific joy of not having anywhere to be today.
When something takes 10 minutes that I've been avoiding for 10 days.
My mouth agreeing to things my brain has already decided against.
Me making an important list on a sticky note that I will never find again.
Running into someone I know while looking exactly like I said I was staying home.
My energy the night before a trip vs. the morning of the trip: Different people.
The second I sit down comfortably: Someone needs something.
Realizing mid-sentence that I have no idea how this thought was going to end.
My brain during the exam: Nothing. My brain after the exam: Everything.
Looking at old photos and being completely unable to remember what was going on.
Preparing a witty comeback and the moment passing before I can deploy it.
Me the moment I figure out how to do something: Teaching it to everyone nearby.
The face I make while pretending to listen to a story I've heard twelve times.
Becoming fully invested in a show I said I'd only watch 'casually.'
Me reading the instructions after I've already assembled the thing incorrectly.
When someone asks if you've tried turning it off and on again. I have now.
My ambition at 9pm making promises my body at 7am refuses to honor.
The disappointment of a meal that looked much better in the photo.
Signing up for a challenge with full commitment and then quietly disappearing.
Falling asleep immediately after promising myself I'd do one more thing.
Me starting over instead of just finishing: An honest autobiography.
The very real effort it takes to pretend I'm okay in a group video call.
Opening 12 tabs with the intention of reading all of them: Closing 12 tabs.
My Sunday night productivity vs. my Monday morning motivation: Never aligned.
Buying ingredients for a healthy week on Sunday. Ordering pizza on Tuesday.
When someone tells you it's 'easy' and it is decidedly not easy.
The specific tiredness that comes from doing nothing all day.
Checking the time to see how much longer I have to be awake.
My handwriting when someone is watching vs. when no one is: Completely different.
The betrayal of getting tired at 9pm and then being wide awake at midnight.
Finishing a great meal and immediately planning the next great meal.
That split second of joy when you find money in a pocket you forgot about.
Me at the end of a productive day: I did one thing. Also me: That counts.
Me during the week: I need to rest. Me on the weekend: *plans 47 things*
My New Year's resolution every January: This year will be different. Every February: No it won't.
The confidence I have when I Google something vs. when someone actually asks me about it.
Me trying to fall asleep at 11pm vs. trying to wake up at 7am: Different people. Different physics.
My reaction to change: Immediate panic. My reaction to the same change a week later: This is fine actually.
When someone gives you 10 tasks at once and smiles like it's a privilege.
My stomach at midnight: Hello. I know we ate. But have you considered: snacks?
Setting a goal on Sunday. Forgetting the goal by Wednesday. Rediscovering it next Sunday.
When I said 'I'll clean up later' what I meant was 'I will look at this mess for two more weeks.'
Being someone who thrives under pressure and someone who creates the pressure for themselves.
Spending 30 minutes looking for something. Finding it immediately once I stop looking.
My attention span vs. a 20-minute meeting that could have been an email.
Me confidently incorrect: presenting wrong information with the energy of a TED Talk.
Standing in the shower having the most productive meeting I'll have all day.
My mouth ordering the complicated thing on the menu and my brain immediately regretting it.
The personal crisis of forgetting what I went to the shop for before I even arrive.
Starting a conversation without knowing where it's going: A lifestyle.
My relationship with exercise: On again, off again but mostly off.
The ten minutes before a deadline turning me into an entirely different, far more capable person.
When I do something ahead of schedule by accident and feel like a completely different human.
Sending an email and immediately worrying I said something wrong in the email.
My sleep vs. any noise that occurs in the house at 3am: I hear all of it.
Being very sure I had a brilliant idea and waking up the next morning with no memory of it.
Organizing everything on my desk so I feel productive without doing any actual work.
Me at the gym for the first time in months: This machine and I have to negotiate.
When I wake up before my alarm and don't know whether to feel proud or betrayed.
My level of commitment to a snack I don't even like because it's already open.
Doing the dishes immediately: Never. Letting them sit until it's a moral dilemma: Always.
The three-second burst of energy I get after saying 'okay I need to get up now.'
Responding to texts: Inconsistent. Overthinking texts I haven't sent yet: Daily.
My brain between 7am and 9am: Out of office.
Buying a healthy snack so I feel virtuous while eating the unhealthy snack.
When the task I've been dreading takes 4 minutes. Four. Minutes.
Waking up from an afternoon nap not knowing what day, year, or planet I'm on.
My relationship with my to-do list: Complicated. I love her but I don't talk to her enough.
Me every Sunday: Tomorrow is going to be the most productive week of my life.
Me every following Monday: Tomorrow for sure.
Completing one thing on my list and treating myself like I ran a marathon.
The amount of courage it takes to check my email after ignoring it for three days.
Telling someone I'll think about it when I've already decided no.
Making big plans with total conviction and then quietly un-planning them.
When someone says the meeting starts at 10 and I arrive at exactly 10 out of breath.
Getting deeply invested in a documentary about something I had zero interest in an hour ago.
The pride of finishing something hard followed immediately by starting something harder.
My multitasking: Doing two things at once and doing both of them badly.
Standing in the shower until the hot water runs out and calling it a difficult day.
The specific joy of hearing your delivery buzzer and running to the door.
When the instructions say 'quick and easy' and it takes me forty-five minutes.
My relationship with healthy food: Admire from a distance. Occasionally commit.
Saying I'll be there in 10 minutes when I haven't left the house yet.
When someone starts a sentence with 'this might be a silly question' and asks something brilliant.
The internal monologue of someone who is pretending to remember something they don't.
Telling yourself the project will take 20 minutes because it's always taken 20 minutes.
My brain solving yesterday's problem perfectly at 2am today.
The very polite panic of not knowing someone's name after too long.
Me at the beginning of a project: I've got this. Me in the middle: Who let me start this.
When you've been on hold so long you're emotionally attached to the hold music.
Describing a recipe I vaguely remember with total authority.
The illusion of productivity that comes from opening 17 browser tabs.
My internal reaction to 'can I speak to you for a second' vs. what my face shows.
Me deciding the one day I'd clean would not be today for the 34th day in a row.
When I eat a vegetable and expect my body to immediately transform.
Saying 'almost there' in the context of my life goals since approximately 2019.
My typing speed when the deadline is real vs. when it's theoretical.
Being completely certain I saved the document. The document: I was never saved.
Me with a plan vs. me when the plan encounters reality.
When someone asks if you're listening and you haven't been listening.
Buying a course to learn something and completing the first chapter three months later.
The internal negotiation of whether it's too late to start something at 11pm. It is not.
Me when someone says 'let's make this fast' — okay but I need to adjust my expectations.
The confidence I project going into Monday that absolutely disappears by Tuesday afternoon.
When you get the perfect gift for someone and they open it differently than you imagined.
Convincing myself I need to buy something and then not buying it and somehow feeling worse.
My brain memorizing useless trivia while forgetting everything I actually need to remember.
The quiet victory of finding something on sale that you needed anyway.
Starting to say something important and trailing off into nothing.
When the thing you were procrastinating turns out to be genuinely simple.
Me and my future self making the same promises every single night.
Choosing the hardest seat at the table to reach because it seemed fine and now it's not fine.
The internal drama of deciding whether to reply now or 'later' — later meaning never.
My confidence at the beginning of a recipe vs. my confidence when it's served.
When someone tells you a 'fun fact' that is actually haunting.
My weekend energy on Friday night vs. Saturday morning: They've never met.
The very specific panic of not having a pen when you desperately need a pen.
Walking into a conversation I was not supposed to overhear and having to look natural.
The commitment I give to doing nothing on my rest day exceeding any other task.
Me buying something I already own because I forgot I owned it.
The joy of a fully charged phone and the betrayal of it dying within the hour.
Saying 'I don't need a basket' at the shop and immediately regretting it.
When you rehearse a call and it goes nothing like you practiced.
My relationship with leftovers: Optimistic when I pack them. Forgetful when I don't eat them.
The very specific satisfaction of finding cash in a bag you haven't used in years.
When the Wi-Fi goes down and I immediately run out of things I wanted to do anyway.
Joining a video call and immediately forgetting what the meeting is about.
Me after one productive hour: I'm a completely different person now. I'm changed.
When someone cancels last minute and I feel such relief I start to worry about myself.
The particular agony of autocorrect changing something correct to something embarrassing.
Going to the market for one thing and coming back with everything except the one thing.
When I think I've been doing something for an hour and it's been four hours. Or twenty minutes.
My Sunday meal prep: Grandiose vision. Monday reality: Takeaway.
Finishing one season of a show and starting the next with reckless disregard for bedtime.
Me every morning: Today I'll be the person I said I'd be last night. Every evening: Interesting.
Looking for parking longer than the errand I came to run actually takes.
Reading the comments section knowing full well it won't help and doing it anyway.
Me responding to stress by reorganizing something that absolutely did not need reorganizing.
The way my to-do list judges me silently across the room.
When the healthy option and the delicious option are never the same option.
Being mid-task and losing all memory of what I was doing mid-task.
The very specific exhaustion that comes only after trying to explain something clearly.
Me when someone says 'what do you want for dinner' — an impossible question with no right answer.
When you clean your inbox and feel like a fundamentally different and better person.
My commitment to not checking my phone lasting approximately as long as it takes to write that sentence.
When someone's advice is technically correct but completely unhelpful for where I am.
The very brave act of asking for help when you've been struggling in silence for too long.
My productivity window: 11:43pm to 12:17am. It's inconvenient but it's mine.
Being the kind of person who finishes tasks early — in a parallel universe where I also do that.
My morning routine vs. what I'm actually doing at 8am: Very different documents.
The sigh that escapes me when I finally sit down after a very long day.
When someone emails me to follow up on an email I forgot to respond to but fully read.
My night-before self promising things my morning self never agreed to.
The moment you realize 'I'll just do a quick tidy' was actually a three-hour commitment.
My internal monologue when I'm running late: Calm. My external presentation when I arrive: Also calm. Somehow.
Feeling completely ready for a conversation and then going fully blank the moment it starts.
When you realize you've been holding your breath while concentrating. For a while.
Me eating the last of something and then immediately wondering what to eat next.
The very specific achievement of closing all your tabs at the end of the day.
When the plan comes together but not at all in the way you planned.
My approach to problems: Worry first, then Google, then accidentally solve it in the shower.
Believing the first thing I read about something and then reading the second thing.
Starting a project with a theme song playing in my head. Finishing it in total silence.
When I remember something important two minutes after I could have done something about it.
My response to unexpected kindness: Visible shock, suspicious delight, eventual gratitude.
The irony of being too tired to sleep.
When your nap was so good it deserves its own highlight reel.
Me telling someone I'll be five minutes and already knowing it will be at least twelve.
My grocery list vs. what I actually need: Occasional overlap.
The very real effort of pretending I knew a word someone just used.
Deciding I'll read more this year and choosing the streaming app instead.
When you realize you've had the solution the whole time and just hadn't looked at it.
My ability to eat something I don't even like simply because it's in front of me.
Sitting down for a moment and then immediately being incredibly comfortable and unable to move.
Me with the best intentions in the world and inconsistent follow-through.
The specific joy of a task finishing itself when you weren't paying attention.
When I've been preparing for a difficult conversation and the person is completely fine.
Being absolutely certain I remembered to do something I absolutely did not do.
My level of investment in other people's problems vs. my own: Wildly disproportionate.
The very brave decision to start again after all of that.
Me planning to be productive: enthusiastic. Me being productive: theoretical.
When the task is huge but you tackle it anyway and feel like a superhero for 20 minutes.
